points.text: SEED Therapeutics — ST-01156 — Neuroblastoma — Clinical-stage RBM39 molecular glue degrader achieved complete tumor eradication in in vivo models; presented at 2026 AACR Annual Meeting.
Erasca announced positive preliminary Phase 1 dose escalation data for ERAS-0015, a pan-RAS molecular glue, demonstrating partial responses in KRAS-mutant solid tumors at doses as low as 8 mg QD.

Erasca announced positive preliminary Phase 1 dose escalation data for ERAS-0015, a pan-RAS molecular glue, showing 62% unconfirmed objective response rate (uORR) in KRAS G12X NSCLC 2L+ and 40% uORR in 2L KRAS G12X PDAC.

SEED Therapeutics — ST-01156 — Neuroblastoma — RBM39 molecular glue degrader demonstrated tumor eradication in vivo; MYC and CDKN2A/B identified as potential response biomarkers in AACR 2026 poster presentation.

Erasca — ERAS-0015 — KRAS-mutant solid tumors — Phase 1 AURORAS-1 trial showed favorable safety, linear PK, and confirmed/unconfirmed partial responses at 8 mg QD; designed as a pan-RAS molecular glue to inhibit RAS signaling and prevent resistance.

Erasca — ERAS-0015 — KRAS-mutant solid tumors — Phase 1 dose escalation data revealed 62% uORR in 2L+ KRAS G12X NSCLC, 75% uORR in post-ICI/platinum 2/3L NSCLC, and 40% uORR in 2L KRAS G12X PDAC; monotherapy well-tolerated with no DLTs.

SEED Therapeutics — Molecular glue degrader development — ST-01156 (RBM39 degrader) + 5 other programs — Clinical-stage biotech pioneering RITE3™ platform; co-founded by Nobel Laureate Avram Hershko and molecular glue pioneer Michele Pagano.

SEED Therapeutics — Molecular glue degrader pipeline expansion — ST-01156 (RBM39 degrader) + 5 other programs — Clinical-stage biotech co-founded by Nobel Laureate Avram Hershko and molecular glue pioneer Michele Pagano; backed by Eli Lilly and Eisai.

Erasca — Precision oncology RAS/MAPK pathway targeting — ERAS-0015 (pan-RAS molecular glue) — Clinical-stage; advancing AURORAS-1 Phase 1 trial with data showing activity across multiple RAS-mutant tumor types.

Erasca — Pan-RAS molecular glue development — ERAS-0015 — Phase 1 AURORAS-1 trial ongoing in US and JYP0015M101 in China; data disclosure for dose expansion and combination cohorts anticipated H1 2027.

ST-01156 — Mechanism: RBM39 molecular glue degrader inducing targeted protein degradation via ubiquitin-proteasome system — Phase: Clinical — Population: Neuroblastoma (in vivo model) — Comparator: Not specified — Results: Complete tumor eradication; MYC and CDKN2A/B proposed as response biomarkers.

ST-01156 — Mechanism: Molecular glue-mediated RBM39 degradation via RITE3™ platform — Phase: Clinical — Population: Neuroblastoma (in vivo model) — Comparator: Not specified — Results: Complete tumor eradication observed; MYC and CDKN2A/B proposed as predictive biomarkers.

ERAS-0015 — Mechanism: Pan-RAS molecular glue inhibiting RAS signaling and wildtype variants to prevent resistance — Phase: 1 (AURORAS-1) — Population: Patients with RAS-mutant solid tumors — Comparator: None stated — Endpoints/Results: Confirmed and unconfirmed partial responses observed at 8 mg QD; favorable safety and linear PK profile.

ERAS-0015 — Mechanism: Pan-RAS molecular glue inducing degradation of RAS proteins — Phase: 1 (AURORAS-1) — Population: KRAS-mutant solid tumors (NSCLC, PDAC, CRC) — Comparator: None (monotherapy data) — Endpoints/Results: 62% uORR in 2L+ KRAS G12X NSCLC, 40% uORR in 2L KRAS G12X PDAC; 1/1 partial response in CRC combination with panitumumab; no DLTs observed.
